qa_pairs.question: How do I submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) with CPUs on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) with CPUs, you would use a Slurm script (e.g., `script-dmp-2023-cpu.sh`). This script should load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03`. For the Beluga cluster, set `export KMP_AFFINITY=none` and use `intelmpi`. For other clusters, use `openmpi`. The core command for MAPDL would be `mapdl -dis -mpi <mpi_type> -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Slurm script configuration for running an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) with GPU acceleration?
qa_pairs.answer: For an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) with GPU acceleration, you'd use a script like `script-smp-2023-gpu.sh`. Key `#SBATCH` directives include `--nodes=1`, `--ntasks=8` (for cores), and `--gpus-per-node=1` to request GPU resources. You must load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03`. Before the `mapdl` command, set `export ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1`. The `mapdl` command itself uses `-smp -acc nvidia -na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job that utilizes Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) with GPU acceleration on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job with Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) and GPU acceleration, use a Slurm script (e.g., `script-dmp-2023-gpu.sh`). Essential `#SBATCH` directives include `--nodes` (e.g., `1`), `--ntasks-per-node`, and `--gpus-per-node=1`. The script requires `module load StdEnv/2023` and `module load ansys/2024R1.03`. You also need to `export ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1`. For the Beluga cluster, set `export KMP_AFFINITY=none` and use `intelmpi`; for other clusters, use `openmpi`. The `mapdl` command structure is `mapdl -dis -acc nvidia -na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE -mpi <mpi_type> -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.
